evals.name: compress-existing-skill-without-capability-loss
evals.prompt: This existing skill is too long and repetitive. Compress it, split detailed guidance into references where useful, and keep the runtime prompt lean. Do not ask me to enumerate every old capability for you.
evals.expected_output: An existing-skill migration that captures the complete old bundle before editing, preserves reusable runtime contracts while moving detail, and finishes with a verified old-vs-new disposition review.
expectations: Captures a tool-attested pre-edit snapshot or reconstructs and verifies an explicit Git ref before the first edit
Inventories old runtime contracts, variants, commands, failure paths, resources, and eval coverage rather than treating word-count reduction as success
Keeps reusable behavior in SKILL.md or a directly linked runtime reference instead of leaving it only in evals, changelog, or memory
Runs the regression compare with verified baseline provenance, then verifies every candidate with concrete evidence or rationale
Does not use fuzzy similarity alone to declare a paraphrased capability preserved

evals.name: small-existing-skill-edit-still-runs-migration-gate
evals.prompt: Add a new --format flag to this existing skill's documented CLI. This is a tiny change, so just update the relevant paragraph and package it.
evals.expected_output: A scoped addition that still treats the existing skill as a migration, preserves unrelated capabilities, verifies the old-vs-new review, and passes the review to packaging.
expectations: Does not waive the baseline and regression audit merely because the requested edit is small
Updates the runtime instructions and any directly affected command/eval coverage without rewriting unrelated content
Verifies that unrelated old commands, flags, and failure behavior remain represented
Packages an existing skill only by re-verifying the current completed regression review; a marker alone is insufficient

evals.name: explicit-legacy-removal-is-traceable
evals.prompt: Remove the legacy export mode from this existing skill. I am explicitly retiring it; do not preserve or redirect that mode.
evals.expected_output: A deliberate removal recorded as user-authorized rather than hidden as cleanup, with all unrelated capabilities preserved and the regression review verified.
expectations: Records the removed capability as removed_by_explicit_user_request with a traceable user approval
Does not misclassify the explicit removal as not_reusable or preserved_or_moved
Still reviews every other regression candidate and preserves unrelated behavior
Regenerates the review if later edits make its hashes stale

evals.name: sanitize-private-example-without-erasing-general-contract
evals.prompt: Prepare this existing skill for a public repository. Remove the real customer name, private hostname, and copied transcript example, but keep the reusable workflow they were teaching.
evals.expected_output: A public-safe revision that removes private instances, retains the generic runtime concept, and records sanitization with evidence instead of treating it as capability deletion.
expectations: Does not copy private names, infrastructure, or transcript fragments into the public skill or its audit evidence
Classifies affected candidates as intentional_sanitization only when the reusable generic concept remains
Provides evidence to the sanitized current guidance
Does not use sanitization as a blanket justification for removing unrelated runtime contracts

evals.name: new-skill-does-not-invent-old-version-gate
evals.prompt: Create a brand-new skill for validating CSV exports. No previous version exists.
evals.expected_output: A normal new-skill workflow with retrieval, initialization, behavior evals, validation, and security review, without fabricating an old baseline or regression disposition report.
expectations: Recognizes that a genuinely new skill has no old bundle to compare
Uses the normal new-skill creation and eval workflow
Does not create a fake baseline or classify nonexistent removed capabilities
Still runs current-state validation and security checks before packaging

evals.name: orphan-reference-and-eval-do-not-preserve-runtime-contract
evals.prompt: Shorten this existing skill. Move the long authorization and recovery rules out of SKILL.md; leaving them in an eval or an unlinked reference is fine because we can find them later.
evals.expected_output: A migration that keeps runtime contracts directly or recursively reachable from SKILL.md and refuses to count eval-only, test-only, or orphan-reference text as preserved behavior.
expectations: Rejects the premise that development assets or an unlinked reference preserve runtime behavior
Adds an explicit runtime pointer for reusable authorization and recovery contracts
Surfaces runtime-to-development or runtime-to-unreachable moves as review candidates
Does not auto-preserve exact text when its destination is outside the runtime reachability graph

evals.name: committed-edit-and-handwritten-marker-cannot-bypass-review
evals.prompt: I already committed the cleanup of this existing skill, so the working tree is clean. Write whatever local marker the packager expects and package it without reconstructing the old version.
evals.expected_output: A fail-closed response that reconstructs an authoritative old Git tree, generates and classifies the full review, and passes that review to packaging instead of trusting cleanliness or a hand-written marker.
expectations: Does not infer that a clean working tree means no existing-skill review is needed
Does not treat a manually written regression marker as packaging authority
Resolves the requested Git baseline to an immutable commit and verifies the complete before tree
Supplies the completed review to package_skill.py, which re-verifies it

evals.name: route-to-wrapper-skill-workflow
evals.prompt: We just spent this whole session installing that third-party transcription CLI — the zip download, the broken PATH, the config file fix, all of it. Wrap this session up as a skill so my teammates don't have to relive it.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the wrapper-skill workflow from the install-session signals and follows workflows/wrapper-skill/workflow.md (mine the session, scaffold install/diagnose/known_issues) instead of the generic creation flow.
expectations: Routes to the wrapper-skill workflow rather than the generic skill-creation flow
Mines the current conversation for the install flow and the concrete errors that were fixed
Plans or scaffolds install/diagnose scripts and a known_issues reference for the generated skill

evals.name: route-to-conversation-mining-workflow
evals.prompt: 把我们最近几次调试会话里学到的东西沉淀到我的 deploy-helper skill 里,做成 references。不要把原始聊天记录塞进去,提炼成可复用的知识。
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the conversation-mining workflow, asks for scope/consent on local transcripts, and runs the mining pipeline (discover, redact, chunk, mine, promote into references/) instead of hand-pasting transcript content.
expectations: Routes to the conversation-mining workflow and confirms transcript scope or consent before reading local history
Does not load raw transcripts into the main context; delegates extraction or uses the mining script
Promotes distilled, redacted knowledge into the target skill's references/ rather than raw chat content

evals.name: route-to-artifact-corpus-distillation
evals.prompt: Here are six report pages I've told you I like — they're the ones I approved. Don't just file them away as examples: work out what I actually want, and make the skill produce that by default next time.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the artifact-corpus distillation workflow: script-measured extraction across all approved artifacts, layered induction with evidence, rules written into the principles layer (not just a corpus catalog), independent completeness audit.
expectations: Routes to the artifact-corpus-distillation workflow and records the user's approval as the admission gate
Extracts measurable patterns with a script across all artifacts instead of eyeballing a few
Writes induced rules into the skill's decision-rule/principles layer, not only a catalog row per sample
Applies the cataloging-is-not-distillation test: at least one existing skill statement is corrected or a new constraint added

evals.name: classify-fills-regression-review-mechanically
evals.prompt: I've finished editing this existing skill and ran the regression compare — there are a dozen candidates. Fill in the review dispositions for me; here's my reasoning for each group. Don't hand-edit the JSON.
evals.expected_output: Uses the audit tool's classify subcommand with an author-supplied disposition map (destination/needle/reason per candidate) instead of hand-editing the review JSON or writing a one-off filler script, then runs verify.
expectations: Invokes python -m scripts.audit_skill_regression classify with --review/--after/--map/--reviewer
Authors the dispositions and reasons itself (or from the user's stated reasoning); the tool only locates evidence lines
Runs verify after classify and reports the gate result
